name: ActionButton
description: Combines a Gravity UI Button with an ActionTooltip, providing a convenient way to add tooltips to action buttons. When no tooltipTitle is provided, it renders as a regular Button without any wrapper.
when_to_use: Toolbar or header action buttons that need tooltips
Icon-only buttons where a tooltip provides context
Replacing plain Button when you want optional tooltip support
avoid: Use plain Button from @gravity-ui/uikit when no tooltip is needed
Do not use for primary call-to-action buttons — use Button with text instead

props.name: children
props.description: Button content (icon, text, or any ReactNode)

props.name: tooltipTitle
props.description: Tooltip title text; omit to render button without tooltip

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the button

props.name: wrapperClassName
props.description: Additional CSS class for the ActionTooltip wrapper

examples:
<ActionButton tooltipTitle="Edit" tooltipPlacement="bottom" view="flat" size="l" onClick={handleEdit}>
  <Icon data={Pencil} size={16} />
</ActionButton>

name: Alert
description: Displays an alert message with an icon indicating its type (default, info, warning, error), an optional action button, and optional collapsible additional content. It provides clear visual feedback for different severity levels in a chat or AI interface.
when_to_use: Showing errors, warnings, or informational messages in a chat interface
Displaying error details that can be expanded/collapsed
Providing a retry action alongside an error message
avoid: For toast/snackbar notifications — use Toaster from @gravity-ui/uikit
For inline form validation messages — use native form error patterns

props.name: content
props.description: Collapsible content displayed below the alert

props.name: icon
props.description: Custom icon to override the default variant icon

props.name: initialExpanded
props.description: Initial expanded state for collapsible content (default: false)

examples: <Alert text="Alert message" button={{ content: 'Retry', onClick: handleRetry }} variant="warning" />
Stack trace...} initialExpanded={true} />

name: AssistantMessage
description: Renders an AI assistant message with support for multiple content parts (text, tool calls, thinking), markdown rendering, and action buttons. Built on top of BaseMessage with assistant-role styling and a pluggable message renderer registry.
when_to_use: Displaying AI assistant responses in a chat interface
Rendering messages with mixed content types (text + tool calls)
Adding copy, like, retry, or custom actions to assistant messages
avoid: For user messages — use UserMessage instead
For tool execution UI — use ToolMessage instead

props.name: content
props.description: Message content as string, single part, or array of parts

props.name: actions
props.description: Array of action buttons (copy, like, retry, etc.)

props.name: id
props.description: Message ID used for generating part keys

props.name: messageRendererRegistry
props.description: Custom renderer registry merged with the default registry

props.name: shouldParseIncompleteMarkdown
props.description: Enable parsing of incomplete markdown during streaming

props.name: showActionsOnHover
props.description: Show action buttons only on hover

props.name: showTimestamp
props.description: Show timestamp in the actions area

props.name: userRating
props.description: Current user rating; fills like/unlike icons when set

examples: <AssistantMessage content="Hello! How can I help you?" />
<AssistantMessage content={{ type: 'text', data: { text: 'Markdown **response**.' } }} actions={[{ type: 'copy', onClick: handleCopy }, { type: 'like', onClick: handleLike }]} showActionsOnHover />
<AssistantMessage content={[{ type: 'text', data: { text: 'First part.' } }, { type: 'tool', data: { toolName: 'Search', status: 'success' } }]} />

name: BaseMessage
description: Base wrapper component for a chat message that supports rendering action buttons (copy, edit, like, retry, delete) with optional hover visibility and timestamp display. It aligns content and actions based on the message role.
when_to_use: Building custom message types that need standard action button behavior
Wrapping any message content with the standard aikit action toolbar
avoid: Prefer AssistantMessage or UserMessage for standard chat messages
Do not add business logic here — keep wrappers thin

props.name: role
props.description: Message role that controls alignment and styling

props.name: actions
props.description: Array of action buttons or ReactNode elements

props.name: showActionsOnHover
props.description: Show actions only on hover (default: false)

props.name: userRating
props.description: Current rating — toggles filled like/unlike icons

examples: <BaseMessage role="assistant" actions={[{ actionType: 'copy', onClick: handleCopy }]} showActionsOnHover>Assistant response</BaseMessage>

name: ButtonGroup
description: A simple flex container for grouping buttons with consistent spacing in horizontal or vertical orientation.
when_to_use: Grouping two or more action buttons together with uniform gap
Building toolbars or action bars in message headers and footers
avoid: For navigation tabs — use the Tabs component instead
When buttons need unequal spacing or complex layout

name: ChatContainer
description: Fully assembled chat page component that integrates Header, ChatContent (EmptyContainer/MessageList), PromptInput, Disclaimer, and History into a complete ready-to-use chat UI. Handles state management, chat switching, streaming support, and welcome screen configuration.
when_to_use: Building a complete AI chat interface with minimal setup
When you need history, input, messages, and header all integrated
Rapid prototyping or production chat applications
avoid: When you need full control over layout — compose individual components instead
For embedding a minimal chat without history or header

props.name: onSendMessage
props.description: Callback when user sends a message

props.name: contentClassName
props.description: CSS class for the content section

props.name: contextItems
props.description: Context items displayed in the prompt input header

props.name: footerClassName
props.description: CSS class for the footer section

props.name: headerClassName
props.description: CSS class for the header section

props.name: hideTitleOnEmptyChat
props.description: Hide header title when chat has no messages (default: false)

props.name: i18nConfig
props.description: I18n configuration for all text labels

props.name: messageListConfig
props.description: Default user/assistant actions and loader statuses

props.name: messages
props.description: Array of messages in the current chat (default: [])

props.name: onClose
props.description: Callback when user closes the chat

props.name: onCreateChat
props.description: Callback when user creates a new chat

props.name: onDeleteAllChats
props.description: Callback when user deletes all chats

props.name: onDeleteChat
props.description: Callback when user deletes a chat

props.name: onFold
props.description: Callback when user folds/unfolds the chat

props.name: onSelectChat
props.description: Callback when user selects a chat from history

props.name: showActionsOnHover
props.description: Show message actions on hover (default: false)

props.name: showClose
props.description: Show close button in header (default: false)

props.name: showFolding
props.description: Show folding button in header (default: false)

props.name: welcomeConfig
props.description: Welcome screen configuration for empty state

examples: <ChatContainer chats={chats} activeChat={activeChat} messages={messages} onSendMessage={handleSend} onSelectChat={setActiveChat} onCreateChat={createChat} />
<ChatContainer messages={[]} onSendMessage={handleSend} welcomeConfig={{ title: 'Welcome!', suggestions: [{ id: '1', title: 'Explain quantum computing' }] }} />

name: ChatContent
description: Template component that switches between an empty welcome state (EmptyContainer) and a message list (MessageList) based on the view prop. Provides a scrollable content area for the main chat body.
when_to_use: As the main content area inside a custom chat layout
When you need automatic switching between empty and chat views
avoid: Use ChatContainer for a fully assembled chat — ChatContent is a building block
Don't use for displaying other types of content outside a chat context

props.name: view
props.description: Display mode: 'empty' for welcome screen, 'chat' for messages

name: ChatDate
description: Displays a formatted date (and optionally time) using a semantic <time> element. Accepts date strings, Date objects, or timestamps; supports relative display (today, yesterday, N days ago) and custom dayjs format strings.
when_to_use: Displaying message timestamps in a chat interface
Showing relative dates like 'today' or 'yesterday' in chat history
Rendering dates in any accessible, semantic format
avoid: For complex date pickers — use DatePicker from @gravity-ui/date-components
For countdown timers — implement custom logic

props.name: date
props.description: Date value in ISO string, Date object, or timestamp format

props.name: relative
props.description: Display relative dates (today, yesterday, N days ago) (default: false)

props.name: showTime
props.description: Show time along with date (default: false)

name: ContextIndicator
description: Circular progress indicator that visualizes AI context window usage as a percentage (0–100%). Accepts either a direct percentage or used/max values, and changes color dynamically based on usage level.
when_to_use: Showing how much of an AI model's context window is consumed
Displaying token or character usage as a visual progress ring
avoid: For general-purpose progress bars — use ProgressBar from @gravity-ui/uikit
For percentage displays without visual ring — use plain text

props.name: type
props.description: Input mode: 'percent' for direct value, 'number' to calculate from used/max

props.name: usedContext
props.description: For 'percent': value 0–100; for 'number': current usage count

props.name: reversed
props.description: Reverse order of indicator and value (default: false)

name: ContextItem
description: A small label that represents a context item (file, selection, etc.) with an optional remove button. Used in the prompt input header to display attached context that can be dismissed.
when_to_use: Displaying removable context items (files, selected text) in the prompt input
Showing tags or labels that can be dismissed by the user
avoid: For read-only tags — use Label from @gravity-ui/uikit directly
For filter chips — use Select or CheckboxGroup

props.name: content
props.description: Content to display inside the label

props.name: onClick
props.description: Callback for the close/remove button; omit to hide button

name: DiffStat
description: Displays compact diff statistics showing the count of added and deleted lines with color-coded +/- prefixes using a monospace font.
when_to_use: Showing code diff statistics in tool messages or code review UIs
Displaying line addition/deletion counts alongside file names
avoid: For full diff rendering — use a dedicated diff component
For general numeric display — use plain text

name: Disclaimer
description: Displays informational or legal disclaimer text with secondary color styling. Accepts either a text prop or custom children, with configurable typography variant.
when_to_use: Showing AI accuracy or legal disclaimers at the bottom of a chat
Displaying informational notices with secondary styling
avoid: For actionable alerts — use Alert instead
For prominent error messages — use Alert with variant='error'

props.name: children
props.description: Custom content alongside or instead of text

examples: <Disclaimer text="AI can make mistakes. Verify important information." />

name: EmptyContainer
description: Template welcome screen component displayed when no chat messages exist. Shows an optional image, title, description, and clickable suggestion buttons to help users get started.
when_to_use: As the welcome/empty state in a new chat session
Providing prompt suggestions to help users start a conversation
avoid: Use ChatContent with view='empty' rather than placing this directly in most cases
For error states — use Alert instead

props.name: image
props.description: Image or icon at the top of the welcome screen

props.name: onSuggestionClick
props.description: Callback when a suggestion is clicked

props.name: showMoreText
props.description: Custom text for the show more button

props.name: wrapText
props.description: Enable text wrapping in suggestion buttons (default: false)

examples: <EmptyContainer title="Welcome to AI Chat" description="Start a conversation" />
<EmptyContainer title="AI Assistant" suggestions={[{ id: '1', title: 'Explain quantum computing' }]} onSuggestionClick={(content) => sendMessage(content)} />

name: Header
description: Chat header component with a title, optional icon, base navigation actions (new chat, history, folding, close), and additional custom actions. Supports title positioning (left/center) and i18n tooltips.
when_to_use: As the top navigation bar in a chat interface
When you need standard chat controls (history, new chat, close) with tooltip support
avoid: For page-level headers — use Gravity UI's standard layout components
Use ChatContainer which includes Header for most cases

props.name: additionalActions
props.description: Custom action buttons appearing before base actions (default: [])

props.name: baseActions
props.description: Predefined actions: NewChat, History, Folding, Close (default: [])

props.name: historyButtonRef
props.description: Ref for history button to anchor popups

props.name: icon
props.description: Icon to the left of the title

props.name: preview
props.description: Preview content after the title (e.g. stage badge)

props.name: showTitle
props.description: Whether to show title and preview (default: true)

props.name: withIcon
props.description: Whether to show the icon area (default: true)

examples: <Header title="AI Chat" baseActions={[HeaderAction.NewChat, HeaderAction.History, HeaderAction.Close]} handleNewChat={createChat} handleHistoryToggle={toggleHistory} handleClose={closeChat} />
<Header title="Chat" preview={<Label>Beta</Label>} baseActions={[HeaderAction.Close]} additionalActions={[{ label: 'Settings', onClick: openSettings }]} handleClose={closeChat} />

name: History
description: Chat history popup component showing a searchable, date-grouped list of past conversations with select and delete capabilities. Supports pagination via button or infinite scroll.
when_to_use: Showing a list of previous conversations in a chat application
Allowing users to switch between past chats with search and grouping
avoid: For in-page lists without popup — compose a custom list
Use ChatContainer which integrates History automatically

props.name: anchorElement
props.description: Anchor element for the popup positioning

props.name: chats
props.description: Array of chat items to display

props.name: emptyFilteredPlaceholder
props.description: Placeholder when search returns no results

props.name: groupBy
props.description: Group chats by date or flat list (default: 'date')

props.name: hasMore
props.description: Whether more chats can be loaded (default: false)

props.name: onDeleteChat
props.description: Callback when a chat is deleted

props.name: onSelectChat
props.description: Callback when a chat is selected

props.name: showActions
props.description: Show delete action on hover (default: true)

props.name: style
props.description: Inline styles including CSS variable overrides

examples: <History chats={chats} anchorElement={anchorRef.current} open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen} onSelectChat={selectChat} onDeleteChat={deleteChat} hasMore={hasMore} onLoadMore={loadMore} searchable groupBy="date" />

name: InlineCitation
description: An atom component for rendering inline text citations within AI assistant messages. Represents a cited source reference inline in the message body.
when_to_use: Rendering inline citations or source references inside AI message content
Linking cited sources within MarkdownRenderer or AssistantMessage output
avoid: For block-level references or footnotes — compose a custom component

name: IntersectionContainer
description: Wrapper component using the Intersection Observer API to trigger a callback when the element enters the viewport. Used for infinite scroll, lazy loading, or scroll-triggered animations.
when_to_use: Triggering loading of previous messages when a user scrolls up in MessageList
Implementing infinite scroll or lazy loading patterns
avoid: For scroll position tracking — use a scroll event listener instead
For animations based on scroll — use CSS scroll-driven animations

props.name: onIntersect
props.description: Callback triggered when element enters viewport

name: InputContext
description: A molecule component for managing and displaying context items in the prompt input area. Shows attached context items (files, selections, etc.) with remove actions in the PromptInputHeader.
when_to_use: Displaying context items attached to the current prompt (files, code selections, search results)
As part of PromptInputHeader to show and allow removal of attached context
avoid: Prefer PromptInputHeader which integrates InputContext automatically
For standalone context indicators — use ContextItem or ContextIndicator directly

name: Loader
description: Visualizes a loading state with two variants: a three-dot streaming animation (for AI text generation) and a circular spinner (for general loading).
when_to_use: Showing streaming text generation with the dots animation
Indicating background loading with the circular spinner
Inside MessageList or ToolFooter to show pending state
avoid: For page-level loading — use Spin from @gravity-ui/uikit directly
For skeleton screens — use Shimmer instead

name: MarkdownRenderer
description: Renders Yandex Flavored Markdown (YFM) content to HTML using the @diplodoc/transform package. Memoized for performance — only re-renders when content or options change.
when_to_use: Rendering AI assistant responses that contain markdown
Displaying documentation or rich text content from YFM sources
avoid: For plain text — use Gravity UI Text component
For user input preview — consider security implications of dangerouslySetInnerHTML

props.name: shouldParseIncompleteMarkdown
props.description: Enable parsing of incomplete markdown during streaming (default: false)

name: MessageBalloon
description: Visual wrapper that renders a styled bubble/balloon for user messages with the info-light background color.
when_to_use: Wrapping user message content in the standard chat bubble style
Building custom user message components that need balloon styling
avoid: For assistant messages — those use plain background without balloon
Use UserMessage which includes MessageBalloon for most cases

name: MessageList
description: Renders a scrollable list of chat messages, automatically choosing between UserMessage and AssistantMessage based on message role. Supports smart auto-scrolling during streaming, loading previous messages with IntersectionContainer, error display, rating block, and custom message renderer registries.
when_to_use: Displaying the full conversation thread in a chat interface
When you need streaming-aware smart scrolling and error handling
avoid: Use ChatContent or ChatContainer which wrap MessageList for most cases
For virtualized very large lists — consider custom virtualization

props.name: hasPreviousMessages
props.description: Whether older messages exist for loading (default: false)

props.name: onRetry
props.description: Callback when user clicks retry in error state

props.name: ratingBlockProps
props.description: Rating block displayed after the message list

props.name: shouldParseIncompleteMarkdown
props.description: Enable parsing of incomplete markdown during streaming

props.name: showActionsOnHover
props.description: Show message actions only on hover

examples: <MessageList messages={messages} hasPreviousMessages onLoadPreviousMessages={loadMore} ratingBlockProps={{ value: rating, onChange: setRating }} />

name: PromptInput
description: Flexible chat input component supporting simple (minimal) and full (with header and footer) views. Handles text input, keyboard shortcuts (Enter to submit, Shift+Enter for newline), file attachments, suggestion display, expandable panels, and streaming cancel.
when_to_use: As the user input area in a chat or AI prompt interface
When you need keyboard shortcuts, status-based button states, and suggestion chips
avoid: For simple text forms — use TextArea from @gravity-ui/uikit
Use ChatContainer which includes PromptInput for most cases
examples: <PromptInput onSend={async (data) => { await sendMessage(data.content); }} bodyProps={{ placeholder: 'Type your message...' }} />
<PromptInput view="full" onSend={handleSend} onCancel={handleCancel} status={status} footerProps={{ showAttachment: true, onAttachmentClick: handleAttach }} />
<PromptInput onSend={handleSend} suggestionsProps={{ showSuggestions: true, suggestions: [{ title: 'Write a poem' }] }} />

name: PromptInputBody
description: Auto-growing textarea body component for the prompt input area. Supports min/max rows, character limit, disabled state, keyboard handling, and custom content replacement.
when_to_use: As the textarea inside a custom prompt input layout
When composing PromptInput from individual parts
avoid: Prefer PromptInput which composes this automatically
For single-line inputs — use TextInput from @gravity-ui/uikit

props.name: children
props.description: Custom content to replace the default textarea

props.name: disabledInput
props.description: Disabled state for the textarea (default: false)

props.name: inputClassName
props.description: Additional CSS class for the textarea control

name: PromptInputFooter
description: Footer section of the prompt input with optional settings, attachment, and microphone action icons plus the SubmitButton. Custom content can be provided while keeping the SubmitButton always visible.
when_to_use: As the footer inside a custom prompt input composition
When you need specific combinations of action icons with the submit button
avoid: Prefer PromptInput which composes this automatically

props.name: children
props.description: Custom content; SubmitButton is always rendered

examples: <PromptInputFooter submitButton={{ onClick: handleSubmit, state: 'enabled' }} showAttachment onAttachmentClick={handleAttach} />

name: PromptInputHeader
description: Header section of the prompt input displaying removable context items on the left and an optional context indicator on the right. Renders nothing if no content is available.
when_to_use: As the header inside a custom prompt input composition
When displaying attached context items or context usage in the input
avoid: Prefer PromptInput which composes this automatically

props.name: children
props.description: Custom content to replace the default header

props.name: contextItems
props.description: Array of context items to display (default: [])

examples: <PromptInputHeader contextItems={[{ id: '1', content: 'file.tsx', onRemove: () => remove('1') }]} showContextIndicator contextIndicatorProps={{ type: 'percent', usedContext: 75 }} />

name: PromptInputPanel
description: Simple panel container for displaying custom content inside the prompt input area, used for expandable top/bottom panels.
when_to_use: As a content container for custom panels above or below the prompt input
Wrapping custom actions, upgrade prompts, or settings in the input area
avoid: Prefer PromptInput which manages panels automatically via topPanel/bottomPanel props

name: RatingBlock
description: Universal rating block with a title and an integrated 5-star rating component. Suitable for CSAT surveys, product reviews, and AI assistant feedback collection.
when_to_use: Collecting user satisfaction ratings for AI assistant responses
Embedding a star rating UI in a chat or feedback form
avoid: For numeric sliders — use Slider from @gravity-ui/uikit
For thumbs up/down — use the like/unlike actions in BaseMessage

props.name: title
props.description: Title text or React element (e.g., with links)

examples: What went wrong? Survey : 'Rate:'} value={rating} onChange={setRating} size="m" />

name: Shimmer
description: Loading skeleton animation component that applies a shimmer gradient effect over its children, indicating that content is being loaded.
when_to_use: Showing a loading placeholder while content is streaming or being fetched
Wrapping text or elements with a shimmer animation during pending states
avoid: For general loading indicators — use Loader instead
For progress bars — use Loader with view='loading'

props.name: children
props.description: Content to apply shimmer effect to

name: StarRating
description: Interactive 5-star rating component with hover preview, keyboard navigation, disabled state, and accessibility support. The building block for RatingBlock.
when_to_use: Collecting a 1–5 star rating from users
When you need just the star input without a title block
avoid: Use RatingBlock when you need a title + stars together
For thumbs up/down — use like/unlike actions

props.name: aria-label-star
props.description: Custom aria-label formatter for individual stars

name: SubmitButton
description: State-driven submit button for chat input that automatically switches icons and tooltips based on state: enabled (arrow up / send), disabled, loading (spinner), and cancelable (stop icon).
when_to_use: As the primary send/cancel button in a chat prompt input
When you need a single button that handles all four input states
avoid: For general purpose buttons — use Button from @gravity-ui/uikit
Prefer PromptInput or PromptInputFooter which include this automatically

props.name: onClick
props.description: Click handler (called only in enabled/cancelable states)

props.name: state
props.description: Button state controlling icon and behavior

props.name: cancelableText
props.description: Optional text displayed in cancelable state beside the stop icon

name: Suggestions
description: Displays a group of clickable suggestion buttons in grid (horizontal) or list (vertical) layout with optional title, icons, and tooltip support.
when_to_use: Presenting pre-defined prompt suggestions in a chat interface
Quick action or follow-up suggestion chips in a conversation
avoid: For multi-select options — use CheckboxGroup or Select
Prefer EmptyContainer or PromptInput which include Suggestions automatically

props.name: items
props.description: Array of suggestion items to display

props.name: onClick
props.description: Callback when a suggestion is clicked

props.name: wrapText
props.description: Enable text wrapping in buttons (default: false)

examples: <Suggestions items={[{ id: '1', title: 'Explain quantum computing' }]} onClick={(c) => sendMessage(c)} layout="grid" />
<Suggestions title="Try asking:" items={[{ title: 'Previous', icon: 'left' }, { title: 'Next', icon: 'right' }]} onClick={handleClick} />

name: Tabs
description: Horizontal tab navigation built on Gravity UI Label components with active state indication, icon support, optional delete functionality, and automatic horizontal scrolling on overflow.
when_to_use: Navigating between multiple chat sessions or conversation threads
Any tabbed interface that needs delete capability and horizontal overflow scroll
avoid: For page-level routing — use router-integrated tab components
For primary navigation — use Gravity UI's Tabs from @gravity-ui/uikit

props.name: items
props.type: Array<{ id: string; title: string; icon?: React.ReactNode }>

props.name: activeId
props.description: ID of the currently active tab

props.name: allowDelete
props.description: Show delete buttons on tabs (default: false)

props.name: onDeleteItem
props.description: Async callback when delete button is clicked

props.name: onSelectItem
props.description: Callback when a tab is clicked

examples: <Tabs items={[{ id: '1', title: 'Chat 1' }, { id: '2', title: 'Chat 2' }]} activeId={activeId} onSelectItem={setActiveId} />

name: ThinkingMessage
description: Displays an AI model's internal reasoning/thinking process as a collapsible block. Shows a loader when actively thinking and a copy button when the thought is complete.
when_to_use: Rendering AI reasoning/chain-of-thought output in a chat
Showing expandable thinking content from models that expose their reasoning
avoid: For regular text messages — use AssistantMessage
For tool execution details — use ToolMessage

props.name: content
props.description: Thinking content as string or array of strings

props.name: enabledCopy
props.description: Enable default copy logic when thought is complete (default: false)

props.name: onCopyClick
props.description: Custom copy handler (takes priority over enabledCopy)

props.name: showStatusIndicator
props.description: Show loader when status is 'thinking' (default: true)

name: ToolFooter
description: Footer component for tool messages that displays a loading indicator, optional shimmer-wrapped status message, and action buttons (e.g. Accept/Reject). Used inside ToolMessage for waiting states.
when_to_use: As the footer inside ToolMessage for waitingConfirmation or waitingSubmission states
When building custom tool card components that need action buttons
avoid: Prefer ToolMessage which manages ToolFooter automatically

props.name: actions
props.description: Array of action buttons to display

props.name: content
props.description: Status message to display with loader

props.name: showLoader
props.description: Whether to show loading indicator (default: true)

examples: <ToolFooter content="Awaiting confirmation" actions={[{ label: 'Accept', onClick: accept, view: 'action' }, { label: 'Reject', onClick: reject, view: 'outlined' }]} />

name: ToolHeader
description: Header component for tool message cards displaying the tool icon, tool name, optional additional content, action buttons, and a status indicator.
when_to_use: As the header inside ToolMessage or a custom tool card component
When building tool execution result cards with icon, name, and actions
avoid: Prefer ToolMessage which manages ToolHeader automatically

props.name: actions
props.description: Array of action buttons to display on the right

props.name: content
props.description: Additional information displayed after tool name

props.name: toolIcon
props.description: Icon displayed next to tool name

examples: <ToolHeader toolName="Writing" actions={[{ label: 'Copy', onClick: copy, icon: <Icon data={Copy} size={16} /> }]} status="loading" />

name: ToolIndicator
description: Status indicator showing different icons based on tool execution status: CircleCheck (success), CircleXmark (error), CircleInfo (info), or a Loader spinner (loading).
when_to_use: Showing tool execution status in ToolHeader or ToolStatus
Any context where a small status icon is needed for tool execution
avoid: For text-based status labels — use ToolStatus which handles 'cancelled' as text
For alert messages — use Alert

name: ToolMessage
description: Complete tool message card component with automatic expand/collapse, status-based footer behavior (waitingConfirmation, waitingSubmission), header/body/footer sections, and auto-collapse on success/cancel.
when_to_use: Rendering AI tool call results or tool execution cards in a chat
Displaying interactive tool states requiring user confirmation or input
avoid: For simple status messages — use Alert
For thinking/reasoning blocks — use ThinkingMessage

props.name: expandable
props.description: Whether the component can expand/collapse (default: Boolean(bodyContent))

props.name: footerActions
props.description: Footer action buttons (auto-generated based on status if omitted)

props.name: footerContent
props.description: Status message in footer (auto-generated based on status if omitted)

props.name: headerContent
props.description: Additional content in header after tool name

props.name: initialExpanded
props.description: Initial expanded state (default: true for waiting statuses)

props.name: onAccept
props.description: Handler for accept action in waitingConfirmation status

props.name: onReject
props.description: Handler for reject action in waitingConfirmation status

examples: file.tsx} onAccept={accept} onReject={reject} />

name: ToolStatus
description: Compound status component that displays a ToolIndicator icon for success/error/loading states, and localized 'Cancelled' text for the cancelled state.
when_to_use: Displaying tool execution status inside ToolHeader or custom tool components
When you need automatic handling of all status variants including cancelled
avoid: Use ToolIndicator directly if you don't need the 'cancelled' text state

name: useAutoCollapseOnCancelled
description: Automatically collapses a tool message panel when its status transitions to 'cancelled'. Accepts an enabled flag, the current tool status, and a setter for the expanded state.
when_to_use: Inside a ToolMessage or custom tool panel that should auto-collapse when the operation is cancelled
When building custom tool UI that mirrors ToolMessage collapse behavior
avoid: Do not use for success-based collapsing — use useAutoCollapseOnSuccess instead
Avoid if you need collapse on multiple status types — write a custom effect instead
signature: useAutoCollapseOnCancelled(options: { enabled: boolean; status: TToolStatus | undefined; setIsExpanded: (expanded: boolean) => void }): void

parameters.name: options.enabled
parameters.description: When false the hook is a no-op

parameters.name: options.status
parameters.description: Current tool status; collapse triggers when value is 'cancelled'

parameters.name: options.setIsExpanded
parameters.description: State setter that the hook calls with false on cancel

name: useAutoCollapseOnSuccess
description: Automatically collapses a tool message panel when its status transitions to 'success'. Mirrors useAutoCollapseOnCancelled but reacts to the success status value.
when_to_use: Inside a ToolMessage or custom tool panel that should auto-collapse once the operation succeeds
When building custom tool UI that mirrors ToolMessage success-collapse behavior
avoid: Do not use for cancellation-based collapsing — use useAutoCollapseOnCancelled instead
Avoid if you need collapse on multiple status types — write a custom effect instead
signature: useAutoCollapseOnSuccess(options: { enabled: boolean; status: TToolStatus | undefined; setIsExpanded: (expanded: boolean) => void }): void

parameters.name: options.enabled
parameters.description: When false the hook is a no-op

parameters.name: options.status
parameters.description: Current tool status; collapse triggers when value is 'success'

parameters.name: options.setIsExpanded
parameters.description: State setter that the hook calls with false on success

name: useDateFormatter
description: Formats a date value into separate date and time strings using dayjs, with support for custom format strings and relative-date thresholds. Returns validation info, a full-precision date string for tooltips, and the underlying dayjs object.
when_to_use: Formatting message timestamps for display in ChatDate or custom date components
When you need both a short display date and a full date string for a title attribute
Parsing and validating dates from API responses before rendering
avoid: Do not use for heavy date arithmetic — use dayjs directly
Avoid when you only need a plain string format — dayjs().format() is simpler

parameters.name: options.format
parameters.description: Optional dayjs format string; defaults to 'YYYY.MM.DD' for date and 'HH:mm' for time

return_shape.formattedDate: string — date portion formatted according to format or default
return_shape.formattedTime: string — time portion formatted according to format or default
return_shape.fullDate: string — locale-aware full date+time string for title attributes
return_shape.dateObject: Dayjs | null — underlying dayjs object, null if invalid
return_shape.isValid: boolean — whether the input date is a valid date
return_shape.diffDays: number | null — days from today, null when beyond RELATIVE_DATE_THRESHOLD

name: useScrollPreservation
description: Preserves scroll position when older messages are prepended to a scrollable container, preventing the visible content from jumping upward. Uses useLayoutEffect to adjust scrollTop synchronously after render.
when_to_use: Inside MessageList or a custom chat container when supporting load-more / infinite scroll upward
Any scrollable list where items are prepended at the top and scroll position must be maintained
avoid: Do not use for downward-appending lists — useSmartScroll handles that case
Avoid when the container is not the direct scroll parent
signature: useScrollPreservation<T extends HTMLElement>(containerRef: RefObject<T>, messagesCount: number): void

parameters.name: containerRef
parameters.description: Ref attached to the scrollable container element

parameters.name: messagesCount
parameters.description: Current total count of messages; the hook responds to increases to detect prepend events

name: useSmartScroll
description: Manages auto-scroll-to-bottom behavior for a chat container, respecting user scroll intent. Automatically scrolls to bottom on mount, new messages, status changes, and DOM mutations during streaming; stops auto-scrolling when the user has scrolled up.
when_to_use: Attaching to the scrollable container inside MessageList or ChatContent
Any chat interface where messages stream in and the view should follow new content unless the user scrolled up
When you need programmatic scrollToBottom for actions like a 'scroll to latest' button
avoid: Do not use for non-chat scrollable lists without a streaming concept
Avoid combining with useScrollPreservation on the same ref without coordination
signature: useSmartScroll<T extends HTMLElement>(options: { isStreaming?: boolean; messagesCount: number; status?: ChatStatus }): UseSmartScrollReturn<T>

parameters.name: options.isStreaming
parameters.description: When true a MutationObserver watches the container for DOM changes and scrolls to bottom

parameters.name: options.messagesCount
parameters.description: Current message count; scroll to bottom fires when this increases

parameters.name: options.status
parameters.description: Chat status; scroll to bottom fires on status change

return_shape.containerRef: RefObject<T> — attach to the scrollable container element
return_shape.scrollToBottom: (behavior?: ScrollBehavior) => void — imperatively scroll to bottom if user has not scrolled up

name: useToolMessage
description: Encapsulates all stateful logic for ToolMessage: expand/collapse state, auto-collapse on success or cancellation, and assembly of header/footer actions including default accept/reject buttons for confirmation flows.
when_to_use: Building a custom tool message UI with the same expand/collapse and action logic as ToolMessage
When you need fine-grained control over tool message state outside of the ToolMessage component
avoid: Do not use when ToolMessage component already covers your needs — prefer the component
Avoid importing useAutoCollapseOnSuccess/useAutoCollapseOnCancelled separately when this hook provides them
signature: useToolMessage(options: ToolMessageProps): { isExpanded: boolean; toggleExpanded: () => void; headerActions: Action[]; footerActions: Action[]; footerContent: React.ReactNode | undefined; showLoader: boolean; isWaiting: boolean }

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Full ToolMessage props object; the hook derives all display state from these props

return_type: { isExpanded: boolean; toggleExpanded: () => void; headerActions: Action[]; footerActions: Action[]; footerContent: React.ReactNode | undefined; showLoader: boolean; isWaiting: boolean }

return_shape.isExpanded: boolean — whether the tool body is currently shown
return_shape.headerActions: Action[] — header actions including expand/collapse chevron when expandable
return_shape.footerActions: Action[] — footer actions; defaults to accept/reject for confirmation statuses
return_shape.footerContent: React.ReactNode | undefined — footer message; defaults to status-based i18n strings
return_shape.showLoader: boolean — true when status is waitingConfirmation or waitingSubmission
return_shape.isWaiting: boolean — true when status is waitingConfirmation or waitingSubmission

name: UserMessage
description: Renders a user message with a styled MessageBalloon wrapper, optional avatar, optional markdown rendering, action buttons, and timestamp support.
when_to_use: Displaying user messages in a chat conversation
When you need avatar, markdown support, and action buttons for user-sent messages
avoid: For assistant messages — use AssistantMessage
Prefer MessageList which renders UserMessage automatically

props.name: content
props.description: Message content (string for plain/markdown, any ReactNode for plain)

props.name: avatarUrl
props.description: URL for the user avatar image

props.name: showActionsOnHover
props.description: Show actions only on hover (default: false)

examples: <UserMessage content="My message" actions={[{ type: 'edit', onClick: handleEdit }]} showActionsOnHover showTimestamp timestamp={message.timestamp} />
